### https://www.linkedin.com/posts/am-ithr_datastrategy-artificialintelligence-dataengineering-activity-7461033434258337792-3zIS
...
Activity: Amy Burden liked this
...
I’ve been noticing a recurring (and slightly worrying) trend in Data and AI hiring lately.
...
More companies are opening up "Head of Data & AI" roles, but there’s a specific phrase popping up in the job descriptions that always rings an alarm bell:
...
"𝒎𝒖𝒔𝒕 𝒃𝒆 𝒉𝒂𝒏𝒅𝒔-𝒐𝒏"
...
When you actually unpack what these organisations are asking for, the list is usually wild.
...
They want a strategic leader to define the AI roadmap, a manager to lead the team, an architect to design the systems, and a developer to actually build the products.
...
That isn't one job. That’s at least three people shoved into one title.
...
Most of the data leaders I talk to are still stuck in the trenches doing the "unsexy" (but vital) foundational work - cleaning up messy data, fixing governance, and modernising legacy architecture that’s been duct-taped together for years.
...
I get it - companies are under massive pressure to show AI results now.
...
But asking one human to fix the foundations, set the high-level strategy, and personally engineer the solution is a recipe for burnout.
...
People will take these jobs because the title sounds exciting, but it begs the question: how do you even define success when the expectations are this fragmented?
...
In reality, most of these companies actually need:  

- Head of Data to get the foundations right.  
- Head of AI to drive the strategy.  

Solid engineering talent to actually build the stuff.
...
Trying to "compress" all of that into one hire might look efficient on a budget spreadsheet, but it’s usually just setting a talented person up to fail.
...
Is the market asking for too much from a single hire right now? #DataStrategy #ArtificialIntelligence #DataEngineering #Leadership

I'll be speaking on an Asset Management panel this Wednesday from 6–9pm alongside top operators and investors in the multifamily space. We're covering the operational strategies that separate high-performing portfolios from the rest:

-Leveraging AI to enhance asset management and operations
-Real-time performance tracking systems that actually drive results
-The specific KPIs that predict portfolio success
-Best practices for executing and managing interior renovations
-Building operational systems that scale with portfolio growth
...
Looking forward to sharing insights on what's working at scale across different portfolio sizes and markets.
...
If you're attending, let's connect in person.
...
And if you're working through any of these operational challenges in your own portfolio, feel free to reach out. Always enjoy discussing the approaches that are working—and the ones that aren't.

results.title: Property Analyst Manager / Lvl 5 / Camden, AR | Wfhforgeon
highlights: Camden Property Analyst Manager for the Asset Management team. Our team is responsible for overseeing property lifecycle, ensuring compliance, and driving operational excellence at our Camden site. What You Will Be Doing As the Camden Property Analyst Manager you will be responsible for leading the Asset Management function, guiding daily operations, and serving as the primary liaison for government and customer agencies. Your responsibilities will include, but are not limited to: Provide strategic Asset Management leadership and guidance over daily operations of the Camden site. Ensure compliance with federal regulations, company policies, and best‑value/operational‑excellence standards. Manage the property life‑cycle for capital, customer, and government owned property. Lead and mentor the onsite team of Asset Management professionals, cultivating a collaborative culture of continuous improvement. Support unique contract requirements for bunker storage, aging, and act as the onsite liaison for audits and stakeholders. Why Join Us The ideal candidate is a results‑driven leader with a proven track record in asset management, strong analytical skills, and a collaborative mindset. This role offers the opportunity to shape critical infrastructure, work with cutting‑edge technologies, and make a tangible impact on national security missions. We are committed to supporting your work‑life balance and overall well‑being, offering flexible scheduling options. Learn more about Lockheed Martin’s comprehensive benefits package here. Further Information About This Opportunity This position is in Camden. Discover more about our Camden, Arkansas location. MUST BE A U.S. CITIZEN - This position requires selected candidate must possess an Secret clearance to start. Basic Qualifications - Prior demonstrated leadership of a team - Demonstrated experience working with Government/Customer representatives, DCMA Property Administrator, DCMA Plant Clearance Officers, Contracting Officers, and other stakeholder teams - Subject matter expert of FAR/DFAR requirements and company policies Desired skills - Previous experience with SAP S/4 HANA, MMS, and other LM used tools - NPMA certification - Lean six sigma certification - Knowledge of GFP Module - Demonstrated problem solver, identifying risk and developing a corrective action plan Lockheed Martin is an equal opportunity employer. Qualified candidates will be considered without regard to legally protected characteristics. The application window will close in 90 days; applicants are encouraged to apply within 5 - 30 days of the requisition posting date in order to receive optimal consideration. • At Lockheed Martin, we use our passion for purposeful innovation to help keep people safe and solve the world's most complex challenges. Our people are some of the greatest minds in the industry and truly make Lockheed Martin a great place to work. With our employees as our priority, we provide diverse career opportunities designed to propel, develop, and boost agility. Our flexible schedules, competitive pay, and comprehensive benefits enable our employees to live a healthy, fulfilling life at and outside of work. We place an emphasis on empowering our employees by fostering an inclusive environment built upon integrity and corporate responsibility. If this sounds like a culture you connect with, you’re invited to apply for this role. Or, if you are unsure whether your experience aligns with the requirements of this position, we encourage you to search on Lockheed Martin Jobs, and apply for roles that align with your qualifications. Other Important Information By applying to this job, you are expressing interest in this position and could be considered for other career opportunities where similar skills and requirements have been identified as a match. Should this match be identified you may be contacted for this and future openings. Ability to work remotely Part-time Remote Telework:

results.title: AI Tech Team Member at UK Asset Manager in London | Chris Halliday posted on the topic | LinkedIn
highlights:
Two spots on an AI Technology team at a leading UK asset manager. Permanent. London This isn't a "we use AI in presentations" role. You'll be building production software from day one; integrating LLMs and GenAI into live systems, working across data pipelines, APIs, and cloud platforms alongside experienced engineers and data scientists. What they're actually looking for: → Strong Python or C# fundamentals → SQL confidence → ML framework exposure: PyTorch, scikit-learn, Pandas → Some understanding of LLMs and RAG in practice → Evidence you build things outside of lectures: GitHub, projects, hackathons 2:1 or above in CS, AI, Maths, Engineering or related field. MSc and PhD candidates very welcome. The honest pitch: this is a serious shop with a genuine AI programme. Not a grad scheme where you spend six months in onboarding before touching anything real. If you're a recent graduate or early-career developer who has been building independently, this is worth a look. Tag someone who should see this, or message me directly. 🔁 A share goes a long way if this is relevant to your network.#GraduateJobs#AIJobs#MachineLearning#SoftwareDevelopment#AssetManagement#GenerativeAI#LondonJobs#TechJobs#Hiring

🚀 Most people build ML models. Few build ML systems that actually work in production. After years working as an ML Engineer & Data Scientist, here's what separates good models from great pipelines: 🔹 Data quality beats model complexity — every time 🔹 Feature engineering is still 80% of the work (yes, even in 2025) 🔹 A model no one can monitor is a model no one can trust 🔹 Real-time inference ≠ batch training — design your architecture accordingly 🔹 LLMs are powerful, but RAG + solid data engineering beats a fancy prompt The gap between a Jupyter notebook and a production ML system is enormous. Bridging that gap is the real skill. I work at the intersection of Machine Learning, Data Engineering & AI — building pipelines that scale, models that generalize, and systems that deliver real business value. Currently based in Berlin 🇩🇪, open to exciting opportunities in AI / ML / Data in Germany. If you work in this space or just want to exchange ideas — feel free to connect! 🤝#MachineLearning#DataEngineering#AIEngineering#DataScience#MLOps#Python#LLM#ArtificialIntelligence#Berlin#OpenToWork

# [Remote] AI Applied Scientist - PhD Intern, Next-Gen Agentic and Multi-Modal Home Exploration Experience

Note: The job is a remote job and is open to candidates in USA. Zillow is dedicated to developing the next generation of agentic systems powered by large foundation models. The AI Applied Science team is seeking remote PhD interns to advance customer-facing experiences and interfaces by building AI agents that handle complex interactions while maintaining context.

- Personalized and Guided Home Exploration: An agentic approach that proactively guides and helps users navigate millions of listings to identify the best homes, the experience should be highly engaging, fun and goal oriented

- Strong research mindset, with motivation to publish
- Interest in applying AI to complex, multi-stakeholder domains

results.title: Senior Data Scientist, Platform (Integrity) (Remote) | Wfh
highlights:
Senior Data Scientist,
...
# Senior Data Scientist, Platform (Integrity) (Remote)
...
Senior Data Scientist, Platform (Integrity)
...
Department: Data Science
...
Trust is the foundation of a vibrant Airbnb community. The Content Integrity Data Science team helps build and protect that trust by ensuring that the content on Airbnb, including listings, profiles, messages and other user-generated experiences, is accurate, authentic and aligned with our policies and community standards.
...
We partner closely with product, engineering, policy and operations teams to develop advanced AI-driven content understanding systems, scale effective human-in-the-loop workflows and proactively defend against emerging content risks, so guests and hosts can interact with confidence.
...
This role is unique in that it directly improves the safety, trust, and quality of real-world user experiences by advancing Airbnb’s ability to understand, interpret and act on content at scale. You will help shape how the platform reasons about listings, profiles, messages and other user-generated content by building the next generation of Trust Content Understanding Models.
...
- The ideal candidate is a motivated and talented “full-stack” Data Scientist with strong applied ML intuition and a bias toward impact, who can own and drive forward challenging, high-visibility initiatives such as:
...
- Advance Airbnb’s content integrity capabilities by building Natural Language Processing (NLP) and LLM-based models that understand intent, policy compliance, quality and risk across listings, profiles, and user communications
...
- Develop high-performing models for detecting problematic or misleading content, including text classification, semantic similarity, information extraction and generative model-based reasoning for policy interpretation and enforcement
...
- Design and optimize human-in-the-loop Machine Learning (ML) systems for content review, labeling, escalation and continuous model improvement
...
- Build systems to detect emerging content risks and abuse patterns across regions, cohorts and surfaces using statistical, ML and representation-learning approaches
...
- Design intelligent sampling and evaluation strategies to measure rare events, policy recall, false positives/negatives and model blind spots in large-scale content systems
...
- Artificial Intelligence / Machine Learning: Build and deploy production AI/ML systems for content integrity and trust content understanding, including feature engineering, model development and evaluation, thresholding, error analysis and end-to-end model lifecycle management. This includes working with NLP and LLM-based models in real production settings.
...
- Inference: Partner with inference data scientists to conduct rigorous quantitative analyses, applying working knowledge of causal inference to interpret results, assess impact, and identify gaps and opportunities to improve content quality and trust outcomes.
...
- 5+ years of industry experience in a quantitative analysis role with a Master’s degree in a quantitative field (computer science, statistics etc.), or 2+ years of experience with a Ph.D.
...
- State-of-the-art knowledge of AI/ML models
- Hands-on experience building, evaluating, and deploying NLP and LLM-based solutions, including text classification, information extraction, semantic understanding or generative applications.
...
This position is US - Remote Eligible. The role may include occasional work at an Airbnb office or attendance at offsites, as agreed to with your manager. While the position is Remote Eligible, you must live in a state where Airbnb, Inc. has a registered entity. Click here for the up-to-date list of excluded states. This list is continuously evolving, so please check back with us if the state you live in is on the exclusion list. If your position is employed by another Airbnb entity, your recruiter will inform you what states you are eligible to work from.

results.title: Data Scientist, GenAI - Atlanta | Great Insurance Jobs
highlights:
Data Scientist, GenAI - Atlanta | Great Insurance Jobs
...
# Data Scientist, GenAI
...
At AIG, we are making long-term investments in a brand-new, innovative Generative AI team, designed to explore new possibilities for how artificial intelligence can be applied in insurance and beyond, and we need your help.
...
With the support and investment needed to explore new frontiers in generative AI, you'll be working alongside talented colleagues, innovating and leading projects that will transform how we manage risk and serve our customers.
...
team is central to our vision of the future and the core of our business offering. We will incorporate best-in-class engineering and product management
...
guidance and collaboration
...
critical to its success.
...
rapidly advance and innovate
...
scale world-class products
...
the opportunity to create meaningful impact
...
we'd love to hear from you.
...
AIG is a leading global insurance organization providing a wide range of property casualty insurance and other financial services. We provide world-class products and expertise to businesses and individuals in approximately 190 countries and jurisdictions.
...
As a Data Scientist at AIG, you will serve a critical role in the architecture, development, and delivery of impactful enterprise-level Generative AI solutions. We are seeking a highly technical individual contributor with extensive hands-on experience, as we are an applied data science team operating in an agile and production-oriented environment. You will be focused on Large Language Models (LLMs) and Machine Learning (ML), acting as a primary technical driver for our most complex projects.
...
End-to-End Development: Lead the development and successful delivery of data science and generative AI solutions in accordance with business requirements.
...
Production Oversight: Monitor solutions in production to ensure performance, reliability, and accuracy.
...
Technical Collaboration: Partner with cross-functional teams including product managers, engineers, and business leaders to translate requirements into technical reality.
...
Evaluation & Quality: Build robust evaluation frameworks to measure LLM efficacy, manage ground truth dataset quality, and guide the product development roadmap.
...
Architecture: Design scalable ML pipelines and RAG frameworks that integrate seamlessly with enterprise data structures.
...
5+ years of experience in a data science role, with a strong emphasis on NLP and ML, working in an agile production-oriented environment.
...
Proven Delivery: Experience successfully delivering multiple GenAI, analytical, or ML projects from conception through to production.
...
Python Expertise: Strong, expert-level proficiency in Python and its data science ecosystem (e.g., PyTorch, Pandas, Scikit-learn).
...
LLM Mastery: 5+ years of practical experience with open-source Large Language Models (Llama 3, Mixtral, etc.), including prompt engineering, inference optimization, and fine-tuning.
...
GenAI Solutions: 3+ years of experience building Generative AI Solutions, including designing and building RAG frameworks, validation pipelines, observability, and monitoring solutions.
...
Education: Master's degree in data science, Computer Science, or a related quantitative field.
...
Palantir Platform Experience: Hands-on experience or certification in the Palantir platform (Foundry/AIP).
...
Ontology Mastery: A strong understanding of Ontology—specifically how to map complex real-world data entities and relationships into a digital twin framework to power AI applications.

results.title: Principal Machine Learning Engineer- LLM Fine-tuning and Optimization | CareerSync
highlights:
Principal Machine Learning Engineer- LLM Fine-tuning and Optimization | CareerSync
...
# Principal Machine Learning Engineer- LLM Fine-tuning and Optimization
...
Machine Learning and Artificial Intelligence are at the heart of the Airbnb product. From Trust to Payments, and from Customer Service to Marketing we rely on ML to ensure that guests and hosts have the best possible experience with Airbnb.
...
The CS AI product team is responsible for driving CSxAI (Customer Support x Artificial Intelligence) initiatives by adopting the Generative AI technologies to enable an intelligent, scalable and exceptional service experience. The team develops and enhances various AI models, ML services and tools including LLM fine-tuning, alignment and optimization, RAG/Search, LLM evaluation and testing automation, feedback-based learning and guardrail for a wide range of applications in Airbnb.
...
As a principal machine learning engineer, you will be responsible for fine-tuning state-of-the-art LLMs for diverse use cases while optimizing models for high-performance deployment on Airbnb’s ML Infrastructure. You will partner with product managers, software engineers, data scientists and operation teams to brainstorm, design and develop AI products such as AI Assistant, Autonomous agent, recommendation, travel planning, and many more products that make meaningful impacts in the world of travel.
...
- Work with large scale structured and unstructured data; explore, experiment, build and continuously improve foundation models for Airbnb product, business and operational use cases.
...
- Create a multi-year tech roadmap that enables our team to stay on the leading edge of the rapidly evolving AI landscape and leverage the best in class technologies to deliver customer benefits.
...
- Continuously evaluate recent and upcoming large foundational models, ensuring the selection and refinement of the highest quality models for enhanced performance and efficiency.
...
- Hands-on prototype, develop and productionize LLM models and pipelines at scale, including both batch and real-time use cases.
...
- Drive key AI architectural decisions for products, and contribute to Airbnb’s ML platform architecture and strategy.
...
- Minimum Qualifications:
...
- PhD in Computer Science, Machine Learning, Mathematics, Statistics, or related technical field.
...
- 10+ years of experience with developing machine learning models and products at scale from inception to business impact.
...
- Programming experience in Python and hands-on experience with frameworks such as PyTorch.
...
- Proven record of training, fine tuning, optimizing models and inference run-time
...
- Post-training experience in areas like data processing for fine-tuning; responsible LLMs; LLM alignment; reinforcement learning; efficient training and inference; language model evaluation; and/or multilingual and multimodal modeling.
...
- Or specialized experience in runtime optimizations, model quantization, compression, on-device inference, GPU inference, pytorch, kernel development
...
- Preferred Qualifications:
...
- PhD in AI, machine learning, data science, or related technical fields.
...
- Publications at peer-reviewed AI conferences (e.g., NeurIPS, CVPR, ICML, ICLR, ICCV, and ACL).
...
- Customer Support Systems: Experience with AI technologies in customer support applications.
...
- Agile Practice for AI production: Experience with the entire AI product development lifecycle from incubation to production at scale, following agile practices in the Applied AI/ML domain.
...
- Infrastructure Acumen: Experience deploying and scaling business-critical AI services and driving architectural requirements on ML infrastructures

results.title: Senior Data Scientist, Platform | Wfhforgeon
highlights:
Senior Data Scientist, Platform | Wfhforgeon
...
# Senior Data Scientist, Platform
...
Trust is the foundation of a vibrant Airbnb community. The Content Integrity Data Science team helps build and protect that trust by ensuring that the content on Airbnb, including listings, profiles, messages and other user-generated experiences, is accurate, authentic and aligned with our policies and community standards.
...
We partner closely with product, engineering, policy and operations teams to develop advanced AI-driven content understanding systems, scale effective human-in-the-loop workflows and proactively defend against emerging content risks, so guests and hosts can interact with confidence.
...
This role is unique in that it directly improves the safety, trust, and quality of real-world user experiences by advancing Airbnb’s ability to understand, interpret and act on content at scale. You will help shape how the platform reasons about listings, profiles, messages and other user-generated content by building the next generation of Trust Content Understanding Models.
...
- The ideal candidate is a motivated and talented “full-stack” Data Scientist with strong applied ML intuition and a bias toward impact, who can own and drive forward challenging, high-visibility initiatives such as:
...
- Advance Airbnb’s content integrity capabilities by building Natural Language Processing (NLP) and LLM-based models that understand intent, policy compliance, quality and risk across listings, profiles, and user communications
...
- Develop high-performing models for detecting problematic or misleading content, including text classification, semantic similarity, information extraction and generative model-based reasoning for policy interpretation and enforcement
...
- Design and optimize human-in-the-loop Machine Learning (ML) systems for content review, labeling, escalation and continuous model improvement
...
- Build systems to detect emerging content risks and abuse patterns across regions, cohorts and surfaces using statistical, ML and representation-learning approaches
...
- Design intelligent sampling and evaluation strategies to measure rare events, policy recall, false positives/negatives and model blind spots in large-scale content systems
...
- A Typical Day:
...
- Artificial Intelligence / Machine Learning: Build and deploy production AI/ML systems for content integrity and trust content understanding, including feature engineering, model development and evaluation, thresholding, error analysis and end-to-end model lifecycle management. This includes working with NLP and LLM-based models in real production settings.
...
- Inference: Partner with inference data scientists to conduct rigorous quantitative analyses, applying working knowledge of causal inference to interpret results, assess impact, and identify gaps and opportunities to improve content quality and trust outcomes.
...
- Empowerment: Think strategically about how to scale and evolve Airbnb’s content integrity defenses, helping define the long-term vision for the role of AI-driven content understanding across the Trust ecosystem.
...
- Your Expertise:
...
- 5+ years of industry experience in a quantitative analysis role with a Master’s degree in a quantitative field (computer science, statistics etc.), or 2+ years of experience with a Ph.D.
- State-of-the-art knowledge of AI/ML models
- Hands-on experience building, evaluating, and deploying NLP and LLM-based solutions, including text classification, information extraction, semantic understanding or generative applications.
...
- Working knowledge of causal inference
...
- Skilled in statistical programming (Python or R) and database usage (SQL)
